title: South Australia decriminalises homosexual acts
description: South Australia became the first Australian jurisdiction to decriminalise male homosexual acts, through the Criminal Law (Sexual Offences) Amendment Act 1975.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Australian Capital Territory decriminalises homosexuality
description: The Law Reform Ordinance 1976 made the ACT the second Australian jurisdiction to decriminalise homosexual acts.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: First Sydney Mardi Gras is held
description: A parade of about 1000 people marched from Taylor Square down Oxford Street toward Hyde Park. Police intervention turned the event into a violent confrontation and multiple arrests.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Victoria decriminalises homosexual acts
description: Victoria decriminalised homosexual acts after the state Labor Party gave unanimous support to the reform bill.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Sydney Mardi Gras moves to summer
description: The Sydney Mardi Gras was shifted from June to the summer season. The 1981 event was postponed to March because of heavy rain, and drew a crowd of more than 5000.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: New South Wales names homosexuality in anti-discrimination law
description: New South Wales amended its Anti-Discrimination Act 1977 to make discrimination on the ground of homosexuality unlawful.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Northern Territory decriminalises homosexuality
description: The Northern Territory's single-chamber Legislative Assembly passed the Criminal Code Act 1983, decriminalising homosexual acts in the territory.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: New South Wales decriminalises homosexual acts
description: Premier Neville Wran secured passage of the Crimes (Amendment) Act 1984 without a conscience vote, decriminalising homosexual acts in New South Wales.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Sydney Mardi Gras adds lesbians to its name
description: The Sydney parade's official name was expanded to include lesbians, becoming the Sydney Gay and Lesbian Mardi Gras.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Western Australia decriminalises homosexuality
description: After four earlier failed attempts, Western Australia passed the Law Reform (Decriminalisation of Sodomy) Act 1989, decriminalising homosexual acts.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Queensland decriminalises homosexual acts
description: Following a Criminal Justice Commission review, Queensland passed the Criminal Code and Another Act Amendment Act 1990 to decriminalise homosexual acts.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Australia lifts ban on gay and lesbian military service
description: After a lesbian servicewoman challenged her dismissal, the Keating Government lifted the ban that excluded gay and lesbian people from serving in the Australian Defence Force.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: ABC televises the Sydney Mardi Gras
description: Despite public criticism of its plans, the ABC broadcast the Sydney Mardi Gras on national television for the first time.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Tasmania decriminalises homosexuality
description: Tasmania, the last Australian state to do so, decriminalised homosexual acts after a reform bill passed the Legislative Council by a single vote and received royal assent.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Netherlands legalises same-sex marriage
description: On 1 April the Netherlands became the first country in the world to open civil marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Sydney Mardi Gras recognised as a hallmark event
description: The Sydney Gay and Lesbian Mardi Gras was formally recognised as a hallmark event.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: New South Wales equalises the age of consent
description: Attorney-General Bob Debus introduced a bill that lowered the male homosexual age of consent to 16, matching the existing heterosexual age of consent.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Belgium legalises same-sex marriage
description: Belgium passed legislation through its Senate and House of Representatives to open marriage to same-sex couples, becoming the second country to do so.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Spain legalises same-sex marriage
description: On 3 July Spain enacted legislation opening civil marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Canada legalises same-sex marriage nationwide
description: After several provinces had already recognised it, Canada extended same-sex marriage across the country on 20 July.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: South Africa legalises same-sex marriage
description: The Civil Union Act came into force in South Africa, opening marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: New South Wales Government funds the Mardi Gras
description: The New South Wales Government announced funding for the Sydney Gay and Lesbian Mardi Gras for the first time in the event's 30-year history.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Norway legalises same-sex marriage
description: Norway's parliament passed a gender-neutral marriage bill that opened marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Sweden legalises same-sex marriage
description: Sweden's parliament passed a gender-neutral marriage bill that opened marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Australia allows transgender military service
description: The Australian Defence Force amended its policy to allow transgender personnel to serve without fear of discharge, following advocacy by soldier Bridget Clinch.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Portugal legalises same-sex marriage
description: Portugal enacted legislation, introduced by the Prime Minister, that opened marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Iceland legalises same-sex marriage
description: On 11 June Iceland passed a gender-neutral marriage law that opened marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Argentina legalises same-sex marriage
description: Argentina became the first country in Latin America to open marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Denmark legalises same-sex marriage
description: A same-sex marriage bill was approved on 7 June and received royal assent from Queen Margrethe II on 12 June, opening marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Rainbow crossing painted on Oxford Street
description: The City of Sydney installed a rainbow pedestrian crossing on Oxford Street.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Brazil legalises same-sex marriage
description: A National Council of Justice decision required every Brazilian state to allow same-sex marriage.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: France legalises same-sex marriage
description: On 18 May France became the 13th country to open marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Uruguay legalises same-sex marriage
description: Uruguay's Chamber of Representatives passed a bill by a large majority to open marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: New Zealand legalises same-sex marriage
description: New Zealand's House of Representatives passed a marriage bill that received royal assent, opening marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: New South Wales expunges historical homosexual convictions
description: New South Wales passed the Criminal Records Amendment (Historical Homosexual Offences) Act 2014, allowing certain past homosexual offences to be expunged.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Same-sex marriage legalised across Great Britain
description: England and Wales were the first to legislate for same-sex marriage, and Scotland followed with its own legislation opening marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Luxembourg legalises same-sex marriage
description: On 18 June Luxembourg's Chamber of Deputies opened marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: United States legalises same-sex marriage nationwide
description: Beginning with Massachusetts in 2004, same-sex marriage spread across the states before being extended nationwide on 26 June.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Ireland legalises same-sex marriage by referendum
description: Ireland held a referendum that amended its constitution to recognise marriage regardless of the sex of the partners.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: New South Wales apologises to the 78ers
description: The New South Wales Government apologised to the 78ers, participants in the 1978 Mardi Gras. The Fairfax media group and NSW Police also issued apologies.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Greenland legalises same-sex marriage
description: Having already recognised registered partnerships, Greenland opened marriage to same-sex couples on 1 April.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Colombia legalises same-sex marriage
description: On 28 April Colombia's Constitutional Court ruled that barring same-sex marriage was unconstitutional, legalising it.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Finland legalises same-sex marriage
description: Finland's parliament approved legislation opening marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Faroe Islands legalise same-sex marriage
description: The Løgting legalised same-sex marriage in the Faroe Islands and allowed same-sex couples to adopt.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Malta legalises same-sex marriage
description: The President of Malta signed a bill opening marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Germany legalises same-sex marriage
description: A same-sex marriage bill passed the Bundestag and the Bundesrat, opening marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Australia legalises same-sex marriage
description: The Australian Parliament passed the Marriage Amendment Act 2017, redefining marriage so that it is no longer limited by sex or gender.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: 40th Sydney Mardi Gras is held
description: Sydney marked the 40th anniversary of its Gay and Lesbian Mardi Gras.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Austria legalises same-sex marriage
description: Austria's Constitutional Court ruled that restricting marriage to opposite-sex couples was unlawful, opening marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Taiwan legalises same-sex marriage
description: Following a Constitutional Court ruling that the existing marriage law was unconstitutional, Taiwan became the first place in Asia to open marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Ecuador legalises same-sex marriage
description: Ecuador's Constitutional Court ruled that the existing marriage law was unconstitutional, leading to the legalisation of same-sex marriage.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019
